title: Numbered Cubbies on the Wall
summary: A shallow wall-mounted row of nine angled cubbies — for mail, papers, or a kid's daily slots. Built from 1x3 and 1x2 with a spacer block for even bays.
description:
This is a shallow wall organizer: a long, low box divided into nine angled cubbies, perfect for sorting mail, holding papers, or giving each kid a numbered daily slot. It's a quick 1x3-and-1x2 build with one clever assembly aid — a scrap spacer block that guarantees every bay comes out the same width.

The dividers are cut with a 45° angle on one end (long-point measurement), so the fronts of the cubbies are open and angled for easy access — you can see and grab what's in each slot. The trick to even spacing is cutting a scrap 1x3 to exactly 4" and using it as a gauge: butt each divider against the block as you fasten it, and all nine bays land at 4" without measuring each one.

Assembly is three passes: dividers to the bottom board, then the back boards, then the front boards, using the 4" block throughout. One practical note from the build: as you fasten near the top, switch from 2" to 1-1/4" nails, because the 45° miter leaves less material and a 2" nail won't have clearance. And don't forget glue. Number the cubbies with paint, vinyl, or little tags, and mount it to the wall studs. A battery brad nailer that needs a hard push can make the divider step fiddly — marking, predrilling, and screwing is a fine alternative.

tools.note: Or predrill and screw if your nailer struggles with the miters.
Hang it level to the studs.

materials.name: 1x3, 8 ft
materials.note: Bottom, dividers, front and back rails.

cutList.note: One end cut at 45°, long-point measurement.

steps.title: Space and attach the dividers
steps.body: Cut a scrap 1x3 to 4" and use it as a spacer block. Nail the nine dividers to the bottom board, spaced 4" apart with the block. If your nailer struggles (a battery nailer that needs a hard push can be awkward here), mark the holes, predrill, and attach with screws instead. Don't forget glue.

steps.title: Attach the back boards
steps.body: Keeping the 4" spacing at the top with your block, attach the back boards to the dividers and bottom with 2" finish nails and glue. As you near the top, switch to 1-1/4" nails — the 45° miter doesn't leave enough material for a 2" nail to clear.

steps.title: Attach the front boards and finish
steps.body: Nail the front boards on, again using the 4" block as a guide so every bay stays even. Sand smooth, finish or paint, and add numbers (paint, vinyl, or tags). Mount to the wall studs, level.

images.alt: Wall-mounted row of numbered angled cubbies
